2606.05658.title: Agent-Orchestrated Adaptive RAG: A Comparative Study on Structured and Multi-Hop Retrieval
2606.05658.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances Large Language Models (LLMs) by grounding their responses in external knowledge, but conventional pipelines rely on static, single-step retrieval that limits performance on complex queries. This paper presents an Agent-Orchestrated Adaptive RAG framework that introduces dynamic query decomposition, iterative retrieval, and a bounded self-reflective evaluation loop. We evaluate the system across two complementary datasets: a domain-specific DevOps knowledge base and the multi-hop reasoning benchmark MuSiQue. Using metrics that include overall score, citation accuracy, mean reciprocal rank, and topic coverage, we find that query decomposition yields consistent gains in the structured domain (overall score $+0.04$, MRR $+0.17$ on DevOps) but degrades ranking precision on the multi-hop benchmark, while the reflection mechanism improves citation accuracy at a substantial latency cost. These contrasting results show that agentic enhancements are not universally beneficial and must be applied selectively according to query and domain characteristics. Our findings argue for adaptive, cost-aware orchestration rather than uniformly aggressive reasoning pipelines.
2606.05658.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）透過將其回應基於外部知識來增強大型語言模型（LLMs），但傳統的流程依賴於靜態的單步檢索，這限制了在複雜查詢上的性能。本文提出了一個代理協調的自適應 RAG 框架，引入了動態查詢分解、迭代檢索和有限的自我反思評估迴圈。我們在兩個互補的數據集上評估該系統：一個特定領域的 DevOps 知識庫和多跳推理基準 MuSiQue。使用包括總分、引用準確性、平均倒數排名和主題覆蓋率在內的指標，我們發現查詢分解在結構化領域中帶來了一致的增益（總分 $+0.04$，在 DevOps 上的 MRR $+0.17$），但在多跳基準上降低了排名精度，而反思機制則在顯著的延遲成本下提高了引用準確性。這些對比結果顯示，代理增強並非普遍有益，必須根據查詢和領域特徵選擇性地應用。我們的研究結果主張應該進行自適應的、考慮成本的協調，而不是統一的激進推理流程。
2606.05658.author: Anuj Maharjan et.al.
2606.05658.authors: Anuj Maharjan, Devinder Kaur, Richard Molyet
